ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No specific SNOMED CT code identified for chicken nuggets as a prepared dish; SNOMED CT coverage for composite fast-food items is limited.

serving.natural_language_portion: One standard serving of 6 chicken nuggets weighs approximately 100 g and is typically consumed as a snack or light meal component.

nutrient_source.title: 6Pc Chicken Nuggets (D) Original — Singapore HPB Foodo Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for 6Pc Chicken Nuggets (D) Original (cr_id G00010019). All core macros and micronutrients are taken verbatim from this record. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA FoodData Central entry for chicken nuggets (USDA SR Legacy #05295) as HPB does not report these fields for this item; values are documented as adapted.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2008 — Atkinson FS, Foster-Powell K, Brand-Miller JC
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database / Diabetes Care Journal
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific HPB-listed chicken nugget product. GI estimated at 66 (medium) based on the closest analogous entry for breaded and fried chicken nuggets from the University of Sydney GI Database (Atkinson et al., 2008, Diabetes Care 31:2281–2283). Glycemic load per serving calculated as: GI × carbohydrate_g per serving / 100 = 66 × 14.3 / 100 = 9.4 per 100 g serving.

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 708 mg per 100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g upper threshold for hypertension-sensitive foods
hypertension.guidance: Individuals managing hypertension should avoid or strictly limit consumption of these chicken nuggets; sodium at 708 mg/100 g substantially elevates daily sodium load, and the potassium content of 250 mg/100 g is insufficient to offset this sodium burden.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: Refined wheat breading raises postprandial glucose; high fat content (22.6 g/100 g) and estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) worsen insulin resistance
type2_diabetes.guidance: People with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid deep-fried breaded chicken nuggets due to the combination of refined carbohydrate coating (GI ~66), high total fat, and estimated trans fat that collectively impair glycaemic control and insulin sensitivity; grilled or baked chicken without breading is a strongly preferred alternative.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: High total fat (22.6 g/100 g), estimated saturated fat (~5.2 g/100 g), estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g), and high sodium (708 mg/100 g) collectively elevate cardiovascular risk
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Individuals with or at risk of cardiovascular disease should avoid regular consumption of these deep-fried nuggets, as the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, and high sodium contributes to dyslipidaemia, endothelial dysfunction, and elevated blood pressure.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates pro-inflammatory advanced glycation end-products (AGEs); estimated trans fat and saturated fat are pro-inflammatory
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried breaded chicken nuggets as the high AGE load from deep-frying, combined with estimated trans fat (0.3 g/100 g) and saturated fat, promotes systemic inflammation and may exacerbate joint symptoms.

llm_training.natural_language_description: 6-Piece Chicken Nuggets Original is a deep-fried, breaded chicken product sold in Singapore, listed in the HPB Foodo database under the identifier G00010019. Each nugget consists of minced or formed chicken meat coated in a seasoned wheat flour breading and deep-fried in vegetable oil, yielding a crispy exterior and a moist, tender interior. The product is halal-certified and commonly consumed as a snack, fast-food meal component, or light lunch or dinner item. Per 100 g, the product delivers 326 kcal, 16.5 g protein, 22.6 g total fat, 14.3 g carbohydrate, and 708 mg sodium. The high sodium content (708 mg/100 g) exceeds the 600 mg/100 g threshold recommended for hypertension-sensitive foods, making this item unsuitable for individuals managing high blood pressure. The refined wheat breading contributes a medium glycemic index (estimated ~66) and raises postprandial glucose, which is a concern for people with Type 2 Diabetes. The estimated trans fat content of approximately 0.3 g/100 g and saturated fat of approximately 5.2 g/100 g elevate cardiovascular and inflammatory risk. Deep-frying also generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs), which are pro-inflammatory and relevant for individuals with rheumatoid arthritis. This product is classified as ultra-processed and is not recommended for regular consumption in health-conscious dietary patterns.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 6 chicken nuggets?
Is chicken nuggets suitable for diabetics?
Sodium content of chicken nuggets Singapore
Can I eat chicken nuggets if I have high blood pressure?
Are chicken nuggets high in fat?

confidence_reason: Core macronutrient and micronutrient values are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010019, confidence 0.95), which is the primary approved source for Singapore food records. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are adapted from USDA SR Legacy chicken nugget data and documented accordingly. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database.
notes: monounsaturated_fat_g and polyunsaturated_fat_g set to -1: detailed fatty acid breakdown not reported in the HPB Foodo database entry for this product, and no exact match found in USDA FoodData Central for this specific Singapore commercial product. water_g set to -1: moisture content not reported in HPB Foodo for this item. vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg set to -1: micronutrient panel not reported in HPB Foodo for this product. trans_fat_g value of 0.3 g/100 g is an estimate based on typical deep-frying conditions for commercial chicken nuggets; HPB does not report trans fat directly. saturated_fat_g of 5.2 g/100 g and dietary_fibre_g of 0.5 g/100 g are adapted from USDA SR Legacy entry for chicken nuggets (USDA #05295) as HPB does not report these fields for this item. GI of 66 is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database for breaded fried chicken nuggets (Atkinson et al., 2008). All core macros (protein_g 16.5, fat_g 22.6, carbohydrate_g 14.3) are non-zero and sourced from HPB; no zero-macro justification required.
